headline: The Delivery Deficit Hiding Inside Domino’s Q1 Earnings
summary: Domino's Pizza Inc. (NASDAQ: DPZ) shares fell after a rare double miss on revenue and earnings revealed a growing delivery deficit. While the market remains fixated on the headline miss, the real story is a sharp decline in delivery demand that is forcing a shift in how Americans buy pizza. This trend led management to slash guidance and sparked a debate: Is this a value trap or a strategic entry point.

content.title: The Delivery Deficit Hiding Inside Domino’s Q1 Earnings
content.summary: Domino's Pizza Inc. (NASDAQ: DPZ) shares fell after a rare double miss on revenue and earnings revealed a growing delivery deficit. While the market remains fixated on the headline miss, the real story is a sharp decline in delivery demand that is forcing a shift in how Americans buy pizza. This trend led management to slash guidance and sparked a debate: Is this a value trap or a strategic entry point.

headline: McDonald's is the latest fast food chain to offer fancier drinks
summary: Fast food chains, thirsty for new ways to grow, are amping up their beverage offerings.  McDonald’s said Tuesday it will launch six crafted beverages in U.S. restaurants on May 6.  It’s joining other chains, like KFC, Wendy’s and Taco Bell, that have elevated their drinks in order to lure customers away from rivals like Starbucks and Dutch Bros.

content.title: McDonald's is the latest fast food chain to offer fancier drinks
content.summary: Fast food chains, thirsty for new ways to grow, are amping up their beverage offerings.  McDonald’s said Tuesday it will launch six crafted beverages in U.S. restaurants on May 6.  It’s joining other chains, like KFC, Wendy’s and Taco Bell, that have elevated their drinks in order to lure customers away from rivals like Starbucks and Dutch Bros.

headline: Starbucks reports stronger than expected quarterly sales as turnaround gains traction
summary: Starbucks said Tuesday that customers are responding to improved service in its stores, which saw better-than-expected sales in the company's fiscal second quarter.  Over the last year, Starbucks has been adding employees to stores during rush times and using technology to better sequence its in-store and mobile orders.  It has also encouraged friendlier service and is redesigning stores to give them a cozier, coffeehouse feel.

content.title: Starbucks reports stronger than expected quarterly sales as turnaround gains traction

Over the last year, Starbucks has been adding employees to stores during rush times and using technology to better sequence its in-store and mobile orders.  It has also encouraged friendlier service and is redesigning stores to give them a cozier, coffeehouse feel.

headline: Starbucks Reports Q2 Fiscal Year 2026 Results
summary: SEATTLE, April 28, 2026--Starbucks Corporation (Nasdaq: SBUX) today reported financial results for its 13-week fiscal second quarter ended March 29, 2026. GAAP results in fiscal 2026 include items that are excluded from non-GAAP results. Please refer to the reconciliation of GAAP measures to non-GAAP measures at the end of this release for more information.

content.title: Starbucks Reports Q2 Fiscal Year 2026 Results
content.summary: SEATTLE, April 28, 2026--Starbucks Corporation (Nasdaq: SBUX) today reported financial results for its 13-week fiscal second quarter ended March 29, 2026. GAAP results in fiscal 2026 include items that are excluded from non-GAAP results. Please refer to the reconciliation of GAAP measures to non-GAAP measures at the end of this release for more information.

headline: Starbucks turnaround plan 'really taking effect,' stock gains on earnings
summary: Coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) is getting a stock lift in Tuesday's extended hours trading after releasing fiscal second quarter results that topped adjusted earnings ($0.50 per share vs. expectations of $0.43) and net revenue estimates ($9.5 billion vs. expectations of $9.14 billion) Placer.ai head of analytical research R.J. Hottovy comes on Asking for a Trend to discuss what the coffeehouse chain's same-store sales growth may signify about the progress in CEO Brian Niccol's turnaround strategy.

content.title: Starbucks turnaround plan 'really taking effect,' stock gains on earnings

headline: Starbucks says higher gas prices aren't yet dampening customers' enthusiasm
summary: Higher gas prices may be changing some consumer spending decisions, but they don't appear to be dampening consumers' appetite for Starbucks.  Starbucks said Tuesday that its global same-store sales for the January-March period rose 6.2%.  Unlike fast food companies, which have been piling on discounts to win back lower-income consumers, Starbucks said it continues to see traffic from people of all ages and income levels.

Starbucks said Tuesday that its global same-store sales for the January-March period rose 6.2%. headline: Big Tech: What this strategist is looking for on earnings calls
summary: US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) closed out Tuesday trading in negative territory, with the Nasdaq Composite leading daily losses as it dropped 0.9%. The pressure may be on for Big Tech as investors await the release of earnings results from Magnificent Seven companies Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Meta Platforms (META), and Microsoft (MSFT) throughout this week. Laffer Tengler Investments CEO and CIO Nancy Tengler joins Brooke DiPalma to talk about the trends she is seeing in the tech and AI landscapes as earnings season picks up.

content.title: Big Tech: What this strategist is looking for on earnings calls
content.description:
US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) closed out Tuesday trading in negative territory, with the Nasdaq Composite leading daily losses as it dropped 0.9%.

The pressure may be on for Big Tech as investors await the release of earnings results from Magnificent Seven companies Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Meta Platforms (META), and Microsoft (MSFT) throughout this week.

Laffer Tengler Investments CEO and CIO Nancy Tengler joins Brooke DiPalma to talk about the trends she is seeing in the tech and AI landscapes as earnings season picks up.
